payload.name: Scope CLI
payload.overview:
# Scope

A local-first kanban for projects, epics, stories, and bugs — designed so coding
agents can plan and track work without leaving the command line. Ships as a CLI,
a GitHub-Projects-style web UI, and a Model Context Protocol server.

## What ships today

- **CLI** — `scope init / project / ticket / epic / link / status / branch / pr / board`,
  with `--json` output on every command for agent consumption.
- **Web UI** (`scope ui`) — kanban columns, drag-and-drop status changes,
  ticket drawer with inline edit, project overview view, epic filter.
- **MCP server** — `scope mcp` (stdio) or via HTTP at `/mcp` when running
  `scope serve`. Exposes 23 tools covering the full CLI surface.
- **`scope serve`** — UI + HTTP MCP on one port. Multiple agents over HTTP and
  a human in the browser all share one SQLite DB; writes from any source push
  to every viewer via SSE.

## Architecture

- **Storage:** SQLite via better-sqlite3, in `.scope/scope.db`. WAL mode for
  safe multi-process writes.
- **CLI:** Node 20+ ES modules, commander for parsing.
- **Server:** Express. Mounts the REST API, the MCP HTTP endpoint, and an SSE
  `/events` channel.
- **Realtime:** in-process EventEmitter bus emits on every mutation; a
  `fs.watch` on `.scope/` plus a `PRAGMA data_version` check catches writes
  from *other* processes (CLI, stdio MCP subprocesses) and feeds them into the
  same bus. UI subscribes via EventSource.
- **MCP:** `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` with zod-typed tool inputs. HTTP mode
  uses StreamableHTTPServerTransport in stateless per-request mode — many
  agents can hit `/mcp` simultaneously, no session affinity needed.

## Install

```
brew install briannadoubt/tap/scope
```

Releases are tag-driven: `npm run release` bumps + tags + pushes; a GitHub
Actions workflow fetches the source tarball, computes its sha256, patches the
formula, and pushes it to `briannadoubt/homebrew-tap`. Zero npm involvement.

## Repo

https://github.com/briannadoubt/scope
